title: Calligraphy Class at the Community Center

all.en: Every Thursday evening, the community center offers a beginner calligraphy class.
all.zh: 每週四晚上，社區活動中心都會開設書法初學者課程。

all.en: I had wanted to try Chinese brush painting for years but never had time.
all.zh: 我多年來一直想嘗試中國毛筆書寫，但始終抽不出時間。

all.en: On the first night, the instructor showed us how to hold the brush properly.
all.zh: 第一堂課，老師示範了如何正確握筆。

all.en: She emphasized that our wrists must stay relaxed and our backs straight.
all.zh: 她強調我們的手腕要放鬆，背要挺直。

all.en: My first attempts at simple strokes looked shaky and uneven on the paper.
all.zh: 我第一次寫的簡單筆畫，在紙上看起來抖抖的、歪歪斜斜。

all.en: The teacher walked around, gently correcting our hand positions one by one.
all.zh: 老師在教室裡來回走動，一個一個輕輕糾正我們的手勢。

all.en: She reminded us that calligraphy is more about breath and patience than speed.
all.zh: 她提醒我們，書法重視的是呼吸與耐心，而不是速度。

all.en: By the third week, my characters started looking more confident and balanced.
all.zh: 到了第三週，我的字開始顯得比較有力、比較平衡。

all.en: I bought my own brush, ink stone, and a small stack of rice paper.
all.zh: 我買了自己的毛筆、硯台，以及一小疊宣紙。

all.en: Practicing at home in silence quickly became my favorite weekend activity.
all.zh: 在家靜靜練字，很快就成了我最喜歡的週末活動。

all.en: It turns out that ancient art forms can still calm a very modern mind.
all.zh: 原來古老的藝術形式，依然能撫慰一顆現代的心。
